task_completion.description: Create a Customer Revenue report view at /admin/commerce/customer-revenue restricted by 'access commerce administration pages'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Drupal Views configuration screen for the 'Customer Revenue' view. The 'Page settings' should show the Path as '/admin/commerce/customer-revenue' and Access as 'Permission | Access commerce administration pages'.

task_completion.description: Enable aggregation and configure fields for Username, Number of Orders (COUNT), and Total Spent (SUM).
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Views UI, 'Use aggregation' must be set to 'Yes'. The 'Fields' section should list the User's Name, Commerce Order ID (with a COUNT aggregation), and Commerce Order Total price (with a SUM aggregation).

task_completion.description: Filter by completed orders and sort by Total Spent descending, 25 items per page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Views UI, the 'Filter criteria' must include 'Commerce Order: Order state (= Completed)'. The 'Sort criteria' must include 'Commerce Order: Total price (desc)'. The 'Pager' section should show 'Paged, 25 items'.

task_completion.description: Add a 'Customer Revenue' link to the Main navigation menu.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the site's main navigation menu (header) or the Menu configuration page showing a link titled 'Customer Revenue' pointing to the created view.

task_completion.description: Create a promotion named 'VIP Reward - [username]' offering 20% off an order of $100 or more.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Commerce Promotions edit screen or list. The name should be 'VIP Reward - [username]' (where [username] is a specific user). The 'Offer' should be 20% off, and the 'Conditions' should require a minimum order total of $100.

task_completion.description: Add a single-use coupon code 'VIP-[USERNAME]' to the promotion.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Coupons section within the promotion. The coupon code must be 'VIP-[USERNAME]' (uppercase, matching the top customer) and the usage limit must be set to 1.

integrity.description: Agent used the Drupal web interface to create the view and promotion.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating and clicking through the Drupal admin UI (Views UI, Commerce Promotions UI) rather than executing drush commands or scripts in a terminal.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual web browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should display a standard web browser interface with the Drupal site loaded, not a text editor modifying database dumps or configuration files directly.

integrity.description: Agent identified the top customer from the generated report rather than guessing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent viewing the completed Customer Revenue report to find the top customer before creating the promotion.
